ground_truth.title: We Tried the 4 Most Popular Clothing Rental Services—Here Are Our Honest Thoughts
ground_truth.author: Emily Cieslak
ground_truth.main_content:
We've rented $2,000 worth of clothes for only $100. With rising prices, looming tariffs, and predictions of an incoming recession, we’re all trying to save money these days. And sadly, splurging on clothes is one of the first things to go when cutting costs. But how’s a girl to keep up with microtrends and clothing cores? Easy—clothing rental services. For just about $100 a month, these sites promise a new wardrobe you can rent or buy at a discount. “Clothing rentals are great for anyone who doesn't want a long-term commitment with clothes but wants something new each time,” says Samantha Dawn, personal stylist and style coach. “Maybe you want to try a new trend, or you have a really special event, but don't want to invest in a piece that you'll only wear once. It's sustainable and budget-friendly and a great way to be experimental.” Here are our honest thoughts about the most popular clothing rental services today. TL;DR
Rent the Runway is best for those who don’t mind paying more for the best rental experience. They carry the largest assortment of brands, offer the most plans and flexibility, and set the bar for customer service.
Nuuly is great for those who want a straightforward rental service for under $100. They carry plus, petite, tall, and maternity sizing, making them the most inclusive rental service as well.
Fashionpass offers the flexibility of RTR for a cheaper price. They carry trendy pieces for both formal and casual occasions.
Armoire lets you rent as many clothes as possible with its Unlimited plan where you swap items as many times as you'd like a month. As Byrdie editors, we always look for new outfits for the office and work events, so we decided to test the most popular clothing rental services to see if they saved us money in the long run. We tested them across body types and personal aesthetics to see how well they cater to different needs. While using them for a month, we paid special attention to the clothing selection and sizes, ease of shipping and returns, and quality of the pieces we received—since no one wants to wear clothing that looks like it’s been passed down by hundreds of people. What to Look for in a Clothing Rental Service
Brands: When choosing between rental services, we think the most important consideration is whether they carry the brands and styles you like. "Always look for services that offer a variety of styles and brands that align with your personal fashion vibe," says celebrity fashion stylist Kim Appelt. "Consider your lifestyle needs—whether it's for work, special events, or casual wear." Most rental services advertise which brands they carry, and you can browse through their styles before signing up to get a feel for the assortment.
Size Options: Likewise, you want to make sure the site carries your sizes, especially if you like to shop plus-size, tall, or petite styles.
Number of Items and Shipments: Before signing up, check out the plans the site offers. Think about how many items you realistically need and will be able to wear a month. If you are going to the office every day and need a new look each time, it can be worth the splurge to pay for a higher membership and receive more shipments per month. Alternatively, if you only want a few items, choose the plan that offers the best value. Best Overall: Rent The Runway
What We Like: Can rent items for as long as you like; Different plan options with multiple shipments a month; Largest assortment of brands (750+).
What to Consider: Have to pay extra for access to full assortment.
Key Specs: Subscription Price: Starting at $79 | Items Per Order: 5 | Top Brands: Tory Burch, Jason Wu, Kate Spade, Ulla Johnson, etc.
How It Works: Known as the OG clothing rental service, Rent the Runway has been in the game for over a decade and offers the most flexibility for renters, hence why we crown it best overall. There are several plans to choose from—starting at $79 a month for five items, all the way to $316 a month for 30 items. The important thing to note is that with all the plans, you can only have five items rented out at a time—so the 30 item plan means you get six shipments of five items a month rather than renting out 30 items at the same time. We appreciate this setup because it allows you to swap items you don’t love or have already worn, rather than having to keep them for the entire month. And if there’s a style you don’t want to send back already? You can technically keep any item you’re renting for as long as you want—it’ll just count towards your five-item quota.
Our Experience: RTR may have gotten a bad rap in the past for being sold out of popular styles or having a stale assortment, but we didn't find this to be the case when testing out the service. We found the site easy to navigate and the assortment curated and fresh. We were able to rent several seasonal dresses and set notifications for popular items we wanted in the future. Nearly all the items came in great condition, with several being brand new. Customer service was excellent. They personally reached out to us to check if we were having a good experience and if everything fit. Shipping only took two days in New York City, and we loved that we could schedule a pickup for the returns. Carrying nearly 800 brands—400 of which are designer—RTR has the largest brand assortment out of all the rentals we tested, making it a great choice for those who want the most comprehensive, flexible rental experience.
Retail Value of Our Orders: $2,632; $1,647.
Tester Quotes: Dominique Hobdy, senior editor: “I was really happy with the pieces I received. I've been really wanting to buy more fun, spring/summer dresses, and renting them just felt like such a great way to get the experience without breaking the bank.” Most Size-Inclusive: Nuuly
What We Like: Most size-inclusive rental service; Straightforward plan; Can buy items at a discount.
What to Consider: Can only swap items once a month; Have to pay extra for shipping.
Key Specs: Subscription Price: $98 | Items Per Order: 6 | Top Brands: Anthropologie, Free People, Urban Outfitters, etc.
How It Works: Nuuly is the rental service from URBN (the parent company of Anthropologie, Free People, and Urban Outfitters). For $98 a month, you get six items. Unlike RTR, you can’t swap items throughout the month—you get one shipment of six items, and you have to keep them for the entire month. However, you can buy any item you love at a discount. The site also carries plus, petite, tall, and maternity sizing, making it the most size-inclusive rental service we tested.
Our Experience: We found the site easy to navigate and the assortment trendy and fresh. We were able to rent several dresses and tops that were perfect for spring. The items came in great condition, and we loved that we could buy them at a discount if we wanted to keep them. Shipping took three days in New York City, and returns were easy—we just had to drop off the package at a UPS location. The only downside is that you can’t swap items throughout the month, so if you don’t like something, you’re stuck with it for the entire month. But if you’re looking for a straightforward rental service that’s size-inclusive and under $100, Nuuly is a great choice.
Retail Value of Our Orders: $1,200; $1,000.
Tester Quotes: Emily Cieslak, associate editor: “I loved the pieces I received from Nuuly. They were trendy and fit well. I also appreciated that they carried plus sizes, which is rare for rental services.” Best for Tall Girlies: Fashionpass
What We Like: Flexible plans; Carries tall sizes; Trendy pieces.
What to Consider: Limited brand assortment; Have to pay extra for shipping.
Key Specs: Subscription Price: Starting at $69 | Items Per Order: 4 | Top Brands: Lulus, Show Me Your Mumu, Tularosa, etc.
How It Works: Fashionpass is a rental service that offers flexible plans starting at $69 a month for four items. Like RTR, you can swap items throughout the month, but you can only have four items rented out at a time. The site carries trendy pieces for both formal and casual occasions, and they also carry tall sizes, which is rare for rental services.
Our Experience: We found the site easy to navigate and the assortment trendy and fresh. We were able to rent several dresses and tops that were perfect for spring. The items came in good condition, but some showed signs of wear. Shipping took four days in New York City, and returns were easy—we just had to drop off the package at a UPS location. The only downside is that the brand assortment is limited compared to RTR and Nuuly, but if you’re looking for a flexible rental service that’s affordable and carries tall sizes, Fashionpass is a great choice.
Retail Value of Our Orders: $800; $600.
Tester Quotes: Emily Cieslak, associate editor: “I loved the pieces I received from Fashionpass. They were trendy and fit well. I also appreciated that they carried tall sizes, which is rare for rental services.” Best Unlimited: Armoire
What We Like: Unlimited swaps; Can rent as many items as you want; Can buy items at a discount.
What to Consider: Limited brand assortment; Have to pay extra for shipping.
Key Specs: Subscription Price: $89 | Items Per Order: Unlimited | Top Brands: Vince, Theory, Rag & Bone, etc.
How It Works: Armoire is a rental service that offers an Unlimited plan for $89 a month. With this plan, you can rent as many items as you want and swap them as many times as you’d like a month. The site carries classic, work-appropriate pieces from brands like Vince, Theory, and Rag & Bone.
Our Experience: We found the site easy to navigate and the assortment classic and work-appropriate. We were able to rent several dresses and tops that were perfect for the office. The items came in good condition, but some showed signs of wear. Shipping took five days in New York City, and returns were easy—we just had to drop off the package at a UPS location. The only downside is that the brand assortment is limited compared to RTR and Nuuly, but if you’re looking for an unlimited rental service that’s affordable and work-appropriate, Armoire is a great choice.
Retail Value of Our Orders: $1,500; $1,200.
Tester Quotes: Emily Cieslak, associate editor: “I loved the pieces I received from Armoire. They were classic and work-appropriate. I also appreciated that I could swap items as many times as I wanted, which made it feel like I had a whole new wardrobe every week.”
with: With rising prices, looming tariffs, and predictions of an incoming recession, we’re all trying to save money these days.
Easy—clothing rental services. For just about $100 a month, these sites promise a new wardrobe you can rent or buy at a discount.
Rent the Runway is best for those who don’t mind paying more for the best rental experience.
Nuuly is great for those who want a straightforward rental service for under $100.
We tested them across body types and personal aesthetics to see how well they cater to different needs.
without: We independently evaluate all of our recommendations. If you click on links we provide, we may receive compensation.
